Title: Expression Patterns and Functional Analysis of 11 E3 Ubiquitin Ligase Genes in Rice

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Gene function was assessed in VIGS-silenced IR64 seedlings; before pathogen inoculation, ROS levels and antioxidant enzyme activities did not differ from controls, indicating that OsPUB39 mainly affects stress-induced rather than basal ROS and defense status.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsPUB39 expression is strongly induced by Magnaporthe grisea infection and by SA, JA, and ACC treatments in rice.
2. BMV-mediated silencing of OsPUB39 in IR64 seedlings causes larger lesions and more fungal growth after M. oryzae inoculation, indicating decreased blast resistance.
3. After pathogen challenge, OsPUB39-silenced plants accumulate more ROS and H2O2 and exhibit increased SOD activity but decreased CAT activity compared with controls.
4. Expression of SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense genes (OsLOX1, OsPR3, OsNH1, OsPR1a) and OsWRKY45 is downregulated in OsPUB39-silenced plants after infection.
5. These data support OsPUB39 as a positive regulator of rice blast resistance, likely acting through modulation of ROS homeostasis and SA/JA/ET-dependent defense gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing OsPUB39 by BMV-based VIGS decreases resistance to Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae, leading to larger lesions, greater fungal growth, higher ROS and H2O2 accumulation, altered SOD and CAT activities, and reduced expression of multiple defense-related genes, indicating that OsPUB39 positively regulates blast resistance via ROS homeostasis and defense-gene activation.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Silencing OsPUB39 enhances pathogen-induced ROS accumulation (higher H2O2), increases SOD activity and reduces CAT activity, and downregulates SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense genes, indicating regulation of ROS-scavenging capacity and defense gene activation during blast infection.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Involved in biotic stress defense pathways linked to SA and JA/ET signaling during rice blast infection.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsPUB39 transcript levels are strongly induced by inoculation with M. grisea in incompatible interactions and by treatment with the signaling hormones SA, JA, and ACC; its expression is not reported as responsive to the tested abiotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae; treatments with SA, JA, and ACC to test hormone responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Rice blast disease resistance and defense signaling via E3 ubiquitin ligase-mediated regulation of ROS and defense-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress – fungal disease (rice blast) resistance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After M. oryzae inoculation, BMV:OsPUB39-infiltrated plants showed significantly larger lesion length and width and higher fungal biomass than BMV:00 controls; H2O2 content increased and CAT activity decreased, whereas SOD activity increased relative to controls (all differences significant at p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Rice (Oryza sativa) cultivars Yuanfengzao, IR64, and isogenic lines H8R/H8S; BMV:OsPUB39-infiltrated (silenced) seedlings; BMV:00-infiltrated control seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Yuanfengzao; IR64; isogenic lines H8R and H8S
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: BLASTP-based identification of rice E3 ubiquitin ligase homologs, BMV-based VIGS in IR64 seedlings, inoculation with M. oryzae spore suspension and detached-leaf disease assay, ROS visualization by DAB staining, H2O2 quantification, SOD and CAT enzyme activity assays, and qRT-PCR of OsPUB39 and defense-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-based virus-induced gene silencing of OsPUB39 in rice cultivar IR64 followed by M. oryzae inoculation, ROS (DAB staining, H2O2) and antioxidant enzyme assays, and qRT-PCR analysis of defense-related gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a rice homolog of a characterized Arabidopsis E3 ubiquitin ligase by BLASTP search of the rice genome using 11 Arabidopsis E3 ligases as queries.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsPUB39 is a positive regulator of resistance to the rice blast fungus Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae. Enhancing OsPUB39 function or expression could be used to increase basal blast resistance in rice breeding, potentially reducing lesion size and pathogen growth without major penalties reported in this study.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Before M. oryzae inoculation, ROS levels and SOD/CAT activities in OsPUB34-silenced seedlings were similar to controls, indicating that OsPUB34 mainly affects stress-induced ROS dynamics and defense activation.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsPUB34 expression is strongly induced by M. grisea infection and by SA, JA, and ACC treatments.
2. BMV-mediated silencing of OsPUB34 increases resistance to M. oryzae, with smaller lesions and reduced fungal biomass relative to controls.
3. After infection, OsPUB34-silenced seedlings accumulate less ROS and H2O2 and show decreased SOD activity but increased CAT activity.
4. SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense genes (OsLOX1, OsPR3, OsNH1, OsPR1a) and OsWRKY45 are upregulated in OsPUB34-silenced plants compared with controls.
5. These results indicate that OsPUB34 acts as a negative regulator of rice blast resistance, likely by modulating ROS-scavenging enzymes and SA/JA/ET-dependent defense gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing OsPUB34 by BMV-based VIGS increases resistance to Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae, resulting in smaller lesions, less fungal growth, reduced pathogen-induced ROS and H2O2 accumulation, decreased SOD activity, increased CAT activity, and elevated expression of several defense-related genes, indicating that OsPUB34 negatively regulates blast resistance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Silencing OsPUB34 reduces pathogen-induced ROS and H2O2 accumulation by lowering SOD activity and enhancing CAT activity, and upregulates SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense-related genes, indicating that OsPUB34 constrains ROS-scavenging enzyme balance and defense gene activation during blast infection.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions in negative regulation of biotic stress responses associated with SA and JA/ET signaling during rice blast infection.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsPUB34 transcript levels are strongly induced by inoculation with M. grisea in incompatible interactions and are also induced by SA, JA, and ACC treatments; expression under the tested abiotic stresses is not reported as significantly changed.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae; treatments with SA, JA, and ACC to assess hormone responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Negative regulation of rice blast disease resistance by a U-box-type E3 ubiquitin ligase and its role in ROS homeostasis and defense signaling.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress – fungal disease (rice blast) resistance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After M. oryzae inoculation, BMV:OsPUB34-infiltrated plants display significantly smaller lesion length and width and lower fungal quantity compared with BMV:00 controls; H2O2 content and SOD activity are reduced, whereas CAT activity is increased (p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Rice (Oryza sativa) cultivars Yuanfengzao, IR64, and isogenic lines H8R/H8S; BMV:OsPUB34-infiltrated (silenced) seedlings; BMV:00-infiltrated control seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Yuanfengzao; IR64; isogenic lines H8R and H8S
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Expression analysis by qRT-PCR after M. grisea inoculation and hormone treatments; BMV-based VIGS of OsPUB34; detached-leaf and whole-plant blast assays; ROS (DAB) staining, H2O2 quantification, SOD and CAT enzyme assays; defense gene expression analysis.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-based virus-induced gene silencing of OsPUB34 in IR64 followed by M. oryzae inoculation, ROS and H2O2 measurements, antioxidant enzyme (SOD, CAT) assays, and qRT-PCR of defense-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a rice homolog of an Arabidopsis E3 ubiquitin ligase by BLASTP search in the rice genome using characterized Arabidopsis E3 ligases as queries.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsPUB34 acts as a negative regulator of resistance to the rice blast fungus. Partial loss of OsPUB34 function via silencing leads to smaller lesions and reduced fungal growth, suggesting that reduced OsPUB34 activity could be exploited in breeding or engineering to enhance rice blast resistance.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsPUB33 silencing did not produce notable effects under heat or salt stress in the conditions tested, and its expression was not clearly induced by ABA, suggesting specificity for blast and drought responses rather than general stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsPUB33 expression is induced by M. grisea infection and by SA, JA, and ACC, and is also rapidly upregulated by drought stress, but not by heat or salt.
2. BMV-mediated silencing of OsPUB33 enhances resistance to M. oryzae, yielding smaller lesions and less fungal growth than in control plants.
3. After blast infection, OsPUB33-silenced plants accumulate less ROS and H2O2, have reduced SOD but elevated CAT activity, and show increased expression of SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense genes and OsWRKY45.
4. Under drought, OsPUB33-silenced plants show increased tolerance, with higher survival, altered water loss, and significantly increased proline and sugar contents and drought-responsive gene expression compared with controls.
5. These data demonstrate that OsPUB33 acts as a negative regulator of both rice blast resistance and drought tolerance, likely via modulation of ROS homeostasis and stress-responsive transcriptional networks.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing OsPUB33 increases resistance to Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae and enhances tolerance to drought stress. After blast inoculation, OsPUB33-silenced plants show smaller lesions, less fungal growth, lower ROS and H2O2 accumulation, decreased SOD but increased CAT activity, and upregulated defense-related genes. Under drought, OsPUB33-silenced plants exhibit higher survival, altered water loss, increased proline and sugar contents, and elevated expression of drought-responsive genes, indicating OsPUB33 is a negative regulator of blast resistance and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: Under blast infection, OsPUB33 silencing reduces ROS and H2O2 accumulation by shifting SOD and CAT activities and enhances expression of SA- and JA/ET-responsive defense genes. Under drought, OsPUB33 silencing increases proline and sugar accumulation and upregulates drought-responsive genes, indicating that OsPUB33 modulates ROS homeostasis and stress-responsive gene expression to negatively regulate blast and drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Involved in biotic stress pathways linked to SA and JA/ET signaling during blast infection and in drought-response pathways associated with proline and sugar accumulation and drought-responsive genes; likely functions in an ABA-independent drought pathway.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsPUB33 expression is strongly induced by M. grisea infection and by SA, JA, and ACC treatments, and its transcript level is also rapidly induced by drought stress; its expression is not significantly altered by heat or salt at the tested time points and is not reported as ABA-responsive.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Inoculation with Magnaporthe grisea/M. oryzae; treatments with SA, JA, and ACC; drought stress imposed by withholding watering of potted plants.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Dual role of an E3 ubiquitin ligase in negatively regulating rice blast resistance and drought stress tolerance via ROS and stress-responsive gene networks.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Biotic stress – fungal disease (rice blast) resistance; Abiotic stress – drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After M. oryzae inoculation, OsPUB33-silenced plants show significantly smaller lesion size and lower fungal biomass than controls; H2O2 content and SOD activity decrease, whereas CAT activity and expression of OsLOX1, OsPR3, OsNH1, OsPR1a, and OsWRKY45 increase (p < 0.05). Under drought, OsPUB33-silenced plants have higher survival rates, altered water loss, and significantly higher proline and sugar contents and drought-responsive gene expression compared with controls (p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Rice (Oryza sativa) cultivars Yuanfengzao, IR64, and isogenic lines H8R/H8S; BMV:OsPUB33-infiltrated (silenced) seedlings; BMV:00-infiltrated control seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Variety: Yuanfengzao; IR64; isogenic lines H8R and H8S
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR expression profiling after M. grisea inoculation, hormone treatments, drought and ABA treatments; BMV-based VIGS of OsPUB33; detached-leaf and whole-plant blast assays; drought assays by withholding water; measurement of ROS, H2O2, SOD, CAT, proline, sugar, survival rate, and water loss; qRT-PCR of defense- and drought-responsive marker genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-based virus-induced gene silencing of OsPUB33 in IR64 followed by M. oryzae inoculation for disease assays and withholding water for drought stress assays, combined with physiological measurements (ROS, H2O2, SOD, CAT, proline, sugar, survival, water loss) and qRT-PCR of defense- and drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a rice homolog of a characterized Arabidopsis E3 ubiquitin ligase by BLASTP search of the rice genome with 11 Arabidopsis E3 ligases as queries.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsPUB33 negatively regulates both resistance to rice blast and tolerance to drought stress. Its silencing enhances blast resistance and drought tolerance, suggesting that reduced OsPUB33 activity, through mutation or targeted downregulation, could be valuable for breeding rice cultivars with improved disease resistance and drought resilience.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsATL69 did not show significant expression induction under cold, heat, or salt in the tested conditions, indicating a more specific role in drought and ABA responses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsATL69 expression is rapidly and strongly induced by drought stress and is also upregulated by ABA treatment.
2. BMV-mediated silencing of OsATL69 decreases drought tolerance, with lower survival rates than controls after a drought and recovery regime.
3. Under drought stress, OsATL69-silenced plants show significantly reduced proline and sugar contents and lower expression of drought-responsive genes compared with controls.
4. These results indicate that OsATL69 positively regulates drought tolerance, likely through ABA-associated pathways that enhance osmoprotectant accumulation and drought-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing OsATL69 by BMV-based VIGS decreases tolerance to drought stress, leading to lower survival rate, reduced proline and sugar contents, and downregulated expression of drought-responsive genes compared with controls, indicating that OsATL69 is a positive regulator of drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsATL69 contributes to drought tolerance by promoting accumulation of osmoprotectants (proline and soluble sugars) and maintaining expression of drought-responsive genes; silencing the gene reduces these responses and compromises drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Associated with ABA-dependent drought-response pathways, as its expression is induced by both drought stress and ABA, and it regulates drought-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsATL69 expression is strongly induced by drought stress within 2 h of treatment and is significantly upregulated by ABA; its expression shows no major changes under cold, heat, or salt stresses at the tested time points.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress applied by withholding watering of 4-week-old potted plants; ABA treatment in hydroponically grown seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Positive regulation of drought stress tolerance in rice by an ATL-type E3 ubiquitin ligase.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under drought stress, BMV:OsATL69-infiltrated plants have significantly reduced survival rate, water loss, proline content, and sugar content compared with BMV:00 controls, and drought-responsive genes are significantly downregulated (p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Rice (Oryza sativa) cultivars Yuanfengzao and IR64; BMV:OsATL69-infiltrated (silenced) seedlings; BMV:00-infiltrated control seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR expression profiling of OsATL69 under drought and ABA treatment; BMV-based VIGS of OsATL69; drought assay by withholding watering of potted plants; measurement of survival rate, water loss, proline and sugar contents; qRT-PCR of drought-responsive marker genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-based virus-induced gene silencing of OsATL69 in IR64 followed by drought treatment by withholding water, with measurement of survival rate, water loss, proline and sugar contents, and qRT-PCR of drought-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a rice homolog of an Arabidopsis ATL-type E3 ubiquitin ligase through BLASTP search of the rice genome using characterized Arabidopsis E3 ligases.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsATL69 positively regulates drought tolerance; silencing it reduces survival, compatible solute accumulation, and drought-responsive gene expression. Maintaining or enhancing OsATL69 activity could be beneficial for breeding drought-tolerant rice cultivars, particularly in environments with frequent water deficit.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: OsATL32 expression was not induced by drought, heat, or salt under the tested conditions, suggesting a more specific function in cold and ABA responses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. OsATL32 expression is strongly induced by cold stress and is also stimulated by ABA treatment.
2. BMV-mediated silencing of OsATL32 markedly decreases cold tolerance, with survival dropping to about 20% compared with over 80% in control plants after a 4°C treatment and recovery.
3. OsATL32-silenced seedlings exhibit higher MDA content and electrolyte leakage and lower chlorophyll content under cold stress, indicating increased membrane and photosynthetic damage.
4. Expression of multiple cold-responsive genes is significantly reduced in OsATL32-silenced plants compared with controls under cold conditions.
5. These results demonstrate that OsATL32 positively regulates cold tolerance in rice, likely via ABA-linked pathways that sustain cold-responsive gene expression and limit cellular damage.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing OsATL32 via BMV-based VIGS reduces tolerance to cold stress, resulting in much lower survival, higher MDA content and electrolyte leakage, reduced chlorophyll content, and downregulated expression of cold-responsive genes compared with controls, indicating that OsATL32 is a positive regulator of cold tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: OsATL32 promotes cold tolerance by limiting membrane damage and maintaining photosynthetic capacity and cold-responsive gene expression; silencing the gene increases lipid peroxidation (MDA), electrolyte leakage, and chlorophyll loss and reduces expression of cold-induced genes.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Associated with ABA-dependent cold-response pathways, as OsATL32 is induced by both cold and ABA and regulates cold-responsive gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: OsATL32 expression is strongly induced by cold stress, with a marked increase 12 h after treatment, and is also significantly upregulated by ABA; it is not reported as responsive to drought, heat, or salt at the tested time points.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress treatment at 4°C for 2 days followed by recovery; ABA treatment in hydroponic seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Positive regulation of cold stress tolerance in rice seedlings by an ATL-type E3 ubiquitin ligase.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: After cold treatment and recovery, BMV:OsATL32-infiltrated plants show a survival rate of 19.62% compared with 83.65% in controls; MDA content and electrolyte leakage are significantly higher, whereas chlorophyll content and expression of cold-responsive genes are significantly lower than in controls (p < 0.05).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Rice (Oryza sativa) cultivars Yuanfengzao and IR64; BMV:OsATL32-infiltrated (silenced) seedlings; BMV:00-infiltrated control seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR expression profiling of OsATL32 under cold and ABA treatments; BMV-based VIGS of OsATL32; cold stress assay by exposure of 4-week-old seedlings to 4°C followed by recovery; measurement of survival, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, chlorophyll content; qRT-PCR of cold-responsive marker genes.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BMV-based virus-induced gene silencing of OsATL32 in IR64 followed by cold treatment at 4°C, then recovery, with assessment of survival rate, MDA content, electrolyte leakage, chlorophyll content, and qRT-PCR of cold-responsive genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a rice homolog of an Arabidopsis ATL-type E3 ubiquitin ligase via BLASTP search with characterized Arabidopsis ligases.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: OsATL32 positively regulates cold tolerance in rice. Maintaining or enhancing OsATL32 activity could be used to improve seedling survival and reduce cellular damage under low temperature, making it a candidate gene for breeding cold-tolerant rice cultivars grown in cooler environments.
